extraction_policy: Every temporal event, time hint, and temporal relation must be supported by evidence from the current narrative-unit text.
Keep discourse order, story/world time, and reveal time separate.
Do not turn script chapter order into hard world-time evidence unless the text provides temporal wording.
Separate plot events, visual context events, forecasts, memories, and hypothetical events using event_track.
Extract a compact temporal layer, not a shot-by-shot action list; prefer 3-6 salient events and never emit more than 8 events or 8 relations for one unit.
For visual-only or subtitle-only montage scenes, emit context events rather than plot events.
For future predictions or plans, emit forecast events and anticipates/claims relations rather than forcing them into the main plot chain.
Mark recalled past events, narrated backstory, flashbacks, plans, forecasts, hypothetical events, montage, and parallel action explicitly.
For memory and forecast events, summarize the remembered or anticipated world event, not only the current speech act that reveals it.
Use relation_type=before as the preferred ordering relation; relation_type=after means source_event_id happens after target_event_id.
Evidence must be one compact verbatim source span from title, subtitle, or content; do not use explanatory paraphrase, ellipses, or stitched multi-span evidence.

absolute_time_hints, relative_time_hints, and temporal_warnings.evidence must cite one compact verbatim source span; if multiple cues exist, pick the clearest one and describe uncertainty in the warning detail.
If the input is Chinese, write natural-language output fields in Chinese except exact codes, acronyms, and official names.
Write all natural-language output values in the same language as the current narrative unit, except exact names, codes, and schema labels.
Assign lower confidence to inferred ordering, ambiguous time jumps, and relations that rely only on weak wording.
The output must be parseable JSON.
